https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pul: English baseline file path derivation uses an overly broad, unanchored regex: `file.getAbsolutePath()...replaceAll("_[a-zA-Z-_]*\\.properties", "_en.properties")`. This can rewrite unintended parts of the path/filename (not just the locale suffix) and can also fail for files without a locale suffix, leading to reading the wrong English baseline or failing to find it.

HTML detection is incorrect for many valid HTML tags because it only matches lowercase opening tags: `Pattern HTML_TAGS = Pattern.compile("<[a-z]+[^>]*>")`. This will miss uppercase/mixed-case tags and closing tags, potentially causing `containsHtml(englishValue)` to return false and then incorrectly applying `POLICY_NO_HTML` to translations.

Anchor sanitization is algorithmically incorrect because it mutates `value` while iterating with a `Matcher` created from the original string, and it removes the first occurrence via `value.replaceFirst(...)` rather than removing the specific match found. Code: `Matcher matcher = ANCHOR_PATTERN.matcher(value); while (matcher.find()) { ... value = value.replaceFirst(Pattern.quote(englishMatcher.group()), ""); }`. This can desynchronize matching and produce incorrect validation results when multiple anchors exist.

Wrong translation introduced: Lithuanian account messages now contain Italian text. Diff: `themes/.../account/messages/messages_lt.properties` changed `totpStep1` to `Installa una delle seguenti applicazioni sul tuo cellulare:`.

Wrong locale variant introduced: Simplified Chinese (zh_CN) account message now uses Traditional Chinese wording/characters. Diff: `themes/.../account/messages/messages_zh_CN.properties` changed `totpStep1` to `在您的手機上安裝以下應用程式之一：`.

verifySafeHtml() throws unchecked RuntimeException on IO failures (including missing English baseline), which will crash the verifier run instead of using the existing `MojoExecutionException` flow. Evidence: `catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException("unable to read file " + englishFile, e); }` (and similarly for `file`).

AccessTokenContext constructor has a copy/paste null-check bug: it checks grantType twice and never checks rawTokenId. In services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/encode/AccessTokenContext.java constructor: `Objects.requireNonNull(grantType, "Null rawTokenId not allowed");` should be `Objects.requireNonNull(rawTokenId, ...)`, otherwise null rawTokenId is not fail-fast and can lead to NPE/malformed token IDs later.

AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId() matcher has inverted logic and wrong substring indices, so it rejects correct token IDs and may accept incorrect ones. In testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/AssertEvents.java: `if (items[0].substring(3, 5).equals(expectedGrantShortcut)) return false;` (a) returns false when it matches (inverted), and (b) uses substring(3,5) but grant shortcut in a 6-char prefix (2 session + 2 token + 2 grant) is at indices [4,6).

AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId() can throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException for shorter/legacy prefixes because it calls `items[0].substring(3, 5)` without checking `items[0].length() >= 5` (or >= 6 for the expected format). This is in testsuite/.../AssertEvents.java and will crash the matcher instead of returning false on unexpected token-id formats.

CredentialHelper.createRecoveryCodesCredential(): possible NullPointerException when provider is missing. The code does `var recoveryCodeCredentialProvider = session.getProvider(CredentialProvider.class, "keycloak-recovery-authn-codes");` and, if `user.credentialManager().updateCredential(...)` returns false, it unconditionally calls `recoveryCodeCredentialProvider.createCredential(realm, user, credentialModel);` without a null check.

RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean: Optional is dereferenced without presence check, causing NoSuchElementException if the user has no recovery-codes credential. Diff shows `Optional<CredentialModel> credentialModelOpt = RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.getCredential(user);` followed by `RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel.createFromCredentialModel(credentialModelOpt.get());`.

Breaking change in public exit-code contract: `CompatibilityResult.RECREATE_UPGRADE_EXIT_CODE` changed from 4 to 3, and 4 is now used for `FEATURE_DISABLED`. This can break external scripts/automation that previously interpreted exit code 4 as “recreate required”. Evidence: `CompatibilityResult.java` diff changes `RECREATE_UPGRADE_EXIT_CODE` from `4` to `3` and adds `int FEATURE_DISABLED = 4;`; commands now call `picocli.exit(CompatibilityResult.FEATURE_DISABLED);` when the feature is disabled.

GroupPermissionsV2.canManage() incorrectly treats VIEW as sufficient for manage-all-groups. In GroupPermissionsV2.java, canManage() returns hasPermission(null, AdminPermissionsSchema.VIEW, AdminPermissionsSchema.MANAGE), so a caller with only VIEW scope on the Groups resource type would pass canManage(), which is used for management operations and should require MANAGE.

UsersResource.searchForUser() removed per-user authorization filtering. In UsersResource.java, the stream is no longer filtered with usersEvaluator::canView (previously: searchForUserStream(...).filter(usersEvaluator::canView)); it now passes the raw searchForUserStream(...) into toRepresentation(...). This can return users the caller cannot view if the underlying user provider does not enforce the session attribute filtering.

BruteForceUsersResource.searchForUser() removed per-user authorization filtering. In BruteForceUsersResource.java, it now passes session.users().searchForUserStream(...) directly to toRepresentation(...) without filtering by usersEvaluator::canView, potentially exposing brute-force/user info for users the caller should not be able to view.

GroupAdapter#getSubGroupsCount() now returns null when modelSupplier.get() returns null, which is a concrete behavior change from always delegating to getGroupModel().getSubGroupsCount(). This can introduce new NullPointerExceptions in callers that unbox the returned Long to long or otherwise assume a non-null count. Evidence in diff: `GroupModel model = modelSupplier.get(); return model == null ? null : model.getSubGroupsCount();`.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: Compilation error in UsernameForm: it calls `isConditionalPasskeysEnabled()` with no arguments, but no such method is shown/added in this diff. The only new method is `UsernamePasswordForm.isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(UserModel user)`. Evidence: `UsernameForm.authenticate(): if (context.getUser() != null && !isConditionalPasskeysEnabled()) {` vs `UsernamePasswordForm: protected boolean isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(UserModel user) { ... }`.

Test logic bug in `PasskeysUsernamePasswordFormTest.reauthenticationOfUserWithoutPasskey()`: it uses `driver.findElement(...)` inside `assertThat(..., nullValue())`, but `findElement` never returns null; it throws `NoSuchElementException`. Additionally, the test catches `Exception`, so the assertion is effectively dead code when the element is absent, and it can also mask unrelated WebDriver exceptions. Evidence (twice in the test): `try { MatcherAssert.assertThat(driver.findElement(By.xpath("//form[@id='webauth']")), nullValue()); fail("Not expected to have webauthn button"); } catch (Exception nsee) { // expected }`.
https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull: Invalid Click option declaration for flusher processes: click.Option is given param_decls ["--flusher-processes", "flusher_processes"], but the second entry lacks leading dashes and will be treated as an option string/flag name by Click (not the internal parameter name). This is likely to break CLI option registration/parsing. Evidence: src/sentry/consumers/__init__.py: click.Option(["--flusher-processes", "flusher_processes"], ...).

Inconsistent metrics tag key for the same shard-list concept: produce timer uses tags={"shard": shard_tag} while wait_produce timer uses tags={"shards": shard_tag}, fragmenting metrics across different tag keys. Evidence: src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: metrics.timer("spans.buffer.flusher.produce", tags={"shard": shard_tag}) vs metrics.timer("spans.buffer.flusher.wait_produce", tags={"shards": shard_tag}).

SpanFlusher can crash with ZeroDivisionError when max_processes is 0 (e.g., CLI passes --flusher-processes=0): num_processes becomes 0 and then process_index = i % self.num_processes will divide by zero when iterating assigned_shards. Evidence: src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: self.num_processes = min(self.max_processes, len(buffer.assigned_shards)); later process_index = i % self.num_processes.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: Replay summarize breadcrumbs: fetch_error_details mis-associates error_ids to nodestore payloads by zipping `error_ids` with `events.values()`. `nodestore.backend.get_multi(node_ids)` returns a dict keyed by node_id, and iterating `.values()` is not guaranteed to align with the original `error_ids` order, so `id/title/message/timestamp` can be paired incorrectly. Evidence: `events = nodestore.backend.get_multi(node_ids)` then `for event_id, data in zip(error_ids, events.values())`.

WidgetCardChart: feature-flagged TableWidgetVisualization branch ignores actual query results and always renders an empty table. When `organization.features.includes('use-table-widget-visualization')` is true, it passes `columns={[]}` and `tableData={{data: [], meta: {fields: {}, units: {}}}}` instead of using `result.data`/`result.meta`/`fields`, so users with the flag will see no table data. Evidence in `static/app/views/dashboards/widgetCard/chart.tsx`.

TableWidgetVisualization: potential runtime crash when `columns` is not provided and `tableData.meta` is undefined. Code does `Object.keys(tableData?.meta.fields)`; optional chaining is only applied to `meta`, not `fields`, so if `meta` is undefined this becomes `Object.keys(undefined.fields)` and throws. Evidence: `Object.keys(tableData?.meta.fields).map(...)` in `tableWidgetVisualization.tsx`.

useTraceItemAttributeKeys: hook can now return `attributes` as `undefined` (initial load or when not fetching and `data` is undefined), whereas previous implementation always returned a `TagCollection` object (at least `{}`). This is a concrete contract change that can break callers doing `Object.keys(attributes)` etc. Evidence: `const {data, isFetching} = useQuery<TagCollection>(...)` and `attributes: isFetching ? previous : data` with no default to `{}`.

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key assumes the ordering key is numeric and calls math.floor/ceil then int() on it. In organization_auditlogs.py the optimized path uses order_by='-datetime' (a datetime field), so get_item_key will attempt math.floor/ceil on a datetime and raise TypeError at runtime. Evidence: organization_auditlogs.py uses paginator_cls=OptimizedCursorPaginator with order_by='-datetime'; paginator.py: `value = getattr(item, self.key)` then `int(math.floor(value) ... else math.ceil(value))`.

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result explicitly slices a Django QuerySet with a negative start index when enable_advanced_features is true and cursor.offset < 0 (`results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop])` where start_offset is negative). Django QuerySet slicing does not support negative indices and raises an error (e.g., 'Negative indexing is not supported'), so this is a concrete runtime crash path. Evidence: paginator.py: `if self.enable_advanced_features and cursor.offset < 0: start_offset = cursor.offset ... results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop])`.

The existing paginator get_result logic was changed to allow a negative start index whenever cursor.is_prev is true: `start_offset = max(0, offset) if not cursor.is_prev else offset`. If offset is negative, this will slice the queryset with a negative start and crash (Django negative slicing unsupported). This is a real regression because Cursor.offset is now explicitly allowed to be negative (cursors.py casts to int with comment allowing negatives). Evidence: paginator.py change at lines around `start_offset = ... else offset` and cursors.py `self.offset = int(offset)` with comment about allowing negative offsets.
https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/: Delete: bad request message is semantically wrong (copy/pasted). Evidence in diff: in server.go Delete(), `if req.ResourceVersion < 0 { return nil, apierrors.NewBadRequest("update must include the previous version") }` — this is a Delete path but the message says "update".

NewResourceServer: calls `s.Init(ctx)` but `ctx` is not defined in the shown function scope, which is a compile-time error as shown in the diff. Evidence: in server.go NewResourceServer(), added `err := s.Init(ctx)` with no `ctx := ...` in the provided diff hunk.
https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull: MetricAlertDetectorHandler now subclasses StatefulDetectorHandler but is an empty `pass` class, so it does not implement required abstract members (e.g., get_dedupe_value, get_group_key_values, build_occurrence_and_event_data, counter_names). Instantiating it will raise `TypeError: Can't instantiate abstract class ...`. Evidence: `class MetricAlertDetectorHandler(StatefulDetectorHandler[QuerySubscriptionUpdate]): pass` in `src/sentry/incidents/grouptype.py`, and new abstract `build_occurrence_and_event_data(...)` plus existing abstract methods in `StatefulDetectorHandler` in `src/sentry/workflow_engine/processors/detector.py`.

StatefulDetectorHandler.evaluate docstring is now incorrect: it still says it returns a list of DetectorEvaluationResult, but the method signature and implementation now return a dict keyed by DetectorGroupKey. Evidence: `def evaluate(...) -> dict[DetectorGroupKey, DetectorEvaluationResult]:` followed by docstring text `returns a list of `DetectorEvaluationResult`` in `src/sentry/workflow_engine/processors/detector.py`.

Test bug: in `test_state_results_multi_group`, the expected second occurrence/event is built with the wrong value (6) even though the input packet has `group_2: 10`, so the expected call args/results are inconsistent with the test input. Evidence: `data_packet = DataPacket(... {"group_1": 6, "group_2": 10})` but `build_mock_occurrence_and_event(..., "group_2", 6, ...)` in `tests/sentry/workflow_engine/processors/test_detector.py`.

incident_occurrence.get_monitor_environment_context computes a modified copy of monitor config (humanizes schedule_type) but returns the original unmodified config, so the computed `config` is unused and the context payload is wrong. Evidence: `config = monitor_environment.monitor.config.copy(); if "schedule_type" in config: config["schedule_type"] = ...; return { ..., "config": monitor_environment.monitor.config, ... }`.

incidents.try_incident_threshold can raise IndexError when `failure_issue_threshold > 1` but fewer than `failure_issue_threshold` check-ins exist: it slices `previous_checkins[:failure_issue_threshold]` (which may be empty) and then unconditionally does `starting_checkin = previous_checkins[0]`.

monitors/types.py adds `class SimpleCheckIn(TypedDict)` using `TypedDict` and `datetime` but the diff shows no corresponding imports added in that module; this will raise NameError at import time if they are not already imported earlier in the file. Evidence: `class SimpleCheckIn(TypedDict): ... date_added: datetime` with no added imports in the shown diff.

AssignmentSource.queued uses a dynamic default evaluated at class definition time (`queued: datetime = timezone.now()`), so all instances created without an explicit queued value will share the same timestamp. This is incorrect for a per-instance creation/queued time and should use `field(default_factory=timezone.now)` instead. Evidence: `src/sentry/integrations/services/assignment_source.py` defines `queued: datetime = timezone.now()`.

AssignmentSource.to_dict() returns a dict containing a raw `datetime` for `queued` (`return asdict(self)`), and this dict is passed into Celery task kwargs (`assignment_source.to_dict()`). A raw datetime is not JSON-serializable under common Celery JSON serializers, which can break task enqueue/serialization at runtime. Evidence: `AssignmentSource.to_dict` uses `asdict`, and `sync_group_assignee_outbound` passes `assignment_source.to_dict()` into `sync_assignee_outbound.apply_async(kwargs=...)`.

Uses Python stdlib `queue.Queue` but calls non-existent shutdown APIs and exception types. In `OrderedQueueWorker.run` it catches `queue.ShutDown`, and in `FixedQueuePool.shutdown` it calls `q.shutdown(immediate=False)`. The stdlib `queue` module/`Queue` do not define `ShutDown` or `Queue.shutdown()`, so shutdown signaling will not work and will raise at runtime. Evidence: `import queue`; `except queue.ShutDown:` in `OrderedQueueWorker.run`; `q.shutdown(immediate=False)` in `FixedQueuePool.shutdown`.

Offsets are marked complete even when processing fails, allowing commits for failed messages (data loss). `OrderedQueueWorker.run` swallows `Exception` from `result_processor` but still calls `self.offset_tracker.complete_offset(...)` in `finally`, and the commit loop commits any completed contiguous offsets. This contradicts the strategy docstring guarantee 'Only commits offsets after successful processing'. Evidence: `except Exception: logger.exception(...)` followed by `finally: self.offset_tracker.complete_offset(work_item.partition, work_item.offset)`; `SimpleQueueProcessingStrategy` docstring states only commits after successful processing.

On submit-path errors, the strategy marks the message offset as completed even though it was not successfully decoded/queued/processed, making it committable and potentially skipping bad messages. Evidence: `SimpleQueueProcessingStrategy.submit` `except Exception:` block calls `add_offset(...)` then `complete_offset(...)` for `message.value.partition`/`offset`.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: Django QuerySet negative slicing will crash at runtime: BasePaginator.get_result now uses `start_offset = max(0, offset) if not cursor.is_prev else offset` and then slices `queryset[start_offset:stop]`. If `cursor.is_prev` and `cursor.offset` is negative, this attempts negative slicing, which Django does not support (raises error). Evidence: src/sentry/api/paginator.py lines changed to allow `start_offset = ... else offset` and `results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop])`.

OptimizedCursorPaginator explicitly slices with a negative start offset when `enable_advanced_features` and `cursor.offset < 0`, which will raise in Django QuerySet slicing. Evidence: src/sentry/api/paginator.py new class: `start_offset = cursor.offset` then `results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop])`.

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key uses math.floor/ceil on the ordered field value, but the endpoint orders by `-datetime` (a datetime object). `math.floor/ceil` on datetime will raise TypeError. Evidence: src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_auditlogs.py uses `order_by="-datetime"` with `OptimizedCursorPaginator`; src/sentry/api/paginator.py: `value = getattr(item, self.key)` then `math.floor(value)`/`math.ceil(value)`.

Lua script references `span_key` but it is not defined in the shown script context, causing a runtime error (nil key) when calling `zcard`, `zunionstore`, and `unlink`. Evidence: src/sentry/scripts/spans/add-buffer.lua: `redis.call("zcard", span_key)` / `zunionstore(..., span_key)` / `unlink(span_key)` with no `local span_key = ...` in the diff context.

Lua ZUNIONSTORE will corrupt scores by default because it aggregates scores with SUM; since scores are timestamps, unioning two sorted sets will add timestamps for duplicate members, breaking ordering/eviction. Evidence: src/sentry/scripts/spans/add-buffer.lua uses `redis.call("zunionstore", set_key, 2, set_key, span_key)` and similarly with parent_key, without specifying `AGGREGATE MIN/MAX`.

Spans consumer requires `end_timestamp_precise` unconditionally; missing field will raise KeyError and break processing for older/variant messages. Evidence: src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/factory.py: `end_timestamp_precise=val["end_timestamp_precise"]` (no default).

Tests will fail at import/collection time: new tests in tests/snuba/api/endpoints/test_organization_events_stats.py use @mock.patch decorators but there is no import for `mock` in the shown diff. Evidence: multiple occurrences of `@mock.patch("sentry.api.helpers.error_upsampling.options")` in that file, but no `import mock` / `from unittest import mock` added in the diff.

Upsampling transformation can break queries against the errors dataset: transform_query_columns_for_error_upsampling rewrites `count()` to `upsampled_count() as count`, and _should_apply_sample_weight_transform returns True for `dataset == errors`, but the PR only registers the `upsampled_count` SnQL function in the Discover dataset (src/sentry/search/events/datasets/discover.py). There is no corresponding registration shown for the errors dataset, so errors-dataset queries using upsampling will likely fail with unknown function. Evidence: src/sentry/api/helpers/error_upsampling.py transforms to `upsampled_count()`, and explicitly enables for `errors`; src/sentry/search/events/datasets/discover.py adds SnQLFunction("upsampled_count", ...), with no similar diff for errors dataset.
https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/: Denial cache key construction is collision-prone because it concatenates unescaped components with '_' separators. In `userPermDenialCacheKey(namespace, userUID, action, name, parent)` the key is built as `namespace + ".perm_" + userUID + "_" + action + "_" + name + "_" + parent`, so different (name,parent) tuples can map to the same key when values contain underscores or are empty, leading to incorrect deny cache hits across different resources/folders. Evidence: `pkg/services/authz/rbac/cache.go` added `userPermDenialCacheKey` with raw concatenation; `service.go` uses it for `permDenialCache` lookups/sets.

Test bug: `TestService_CacheCheck` 'Should deny on explicit cache deny entry' does not actually validate that `permDenialCache` overrides an allow, because it sets the permission cache value to `false` while the comment says it is allowing access. As written, the test can still pass even if the denial cache is ignored, since `permCache` already denies. Evidence: `pkg/services/authz/rbac/service_test.go` comment `// Allow access to the dashboard to prove this is not checked` followed by `map[string]bool{"dashboards:uid:dash1": false}`.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pul: OrganizationCacheTest.testCacheIDPForLogin registers cleanup for the wrong IDP alias: it uses the literal "alias" instead of the created alias ("idp-alias-" + i / "idp-alias-20"), so created IDPs may not be cleaned up and it may attempt to remove a non-existent provider. Evidence: `getCleanup().addCleanup(testRealm().identityProviders().get("alias")::remove);` appears right after creating IDPs in the loop and again after creating idp-alias-20.

IdentityProviderStorageProvider.LoginFilter.getLoginPredicate() can throw NullPointerException when an IDP has a non-null organizationId but `idp.getConfig()` is null, because it unconditionally calls `idp.getConfig().get(...)`. Evidence: `.and(idp -> idp.getOrganizationId() == null || Boolean.parseBoolean(idp.getConfig().get(OrganizationModel.BROKER_PUBLIC)))`.
https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/:
Misleading error message when SQL expressions are disabled: it returns "sqlExpressions is not implemented" rather than indicating the feature is disabled/not enabled. Evidence (pkg/expr/reader.go):
if !enabled { return eq, fmt.Errorf("sqlExpressions is not implemented") }

New sql DB implementation is stubbed (RunCommands/QueryFramesInto/TablesList return "not implemented"), but production code paths call these methods, causing guaranteed runtime errors if reached. Evidence: pkg/expr/sql/db.go methods all return errors.New("not implemented"); called from pkg/expr/sql/parser.go (duckDB.RunCommands) and pkg/expr/sql_command.go (db.QueryFramesInto).

SQLCommand.Execute now references sql.NewInMemoryDB() but the diff shows the prior duck import removed and no new import added for the new package, so `sql` is undefined and this will not compile. Evidence (pkg/expr/sql_command.go): removed `github.com/scottlepp/go-duck/duck` import; code now uses `db := sql.NewInMemoryDB()` with no shown import for `pkg/expr/sql`.

SQLCommand.Execute returns an undefined identifier `r` on the error path, causing a compile-time failure. Evidence (pkg/expr/sql_command.go):
if err != nil { ... rsp.Error = err
  return r
}

Cache recomputation under write lock without re-check: after the initial RLock/RUnlock read, the code acquires entryPointAssetsCacheMu.Lock() and proceeds without re-checking whether entryPointAssetsCache was populated while waiting for the write lock. Evidence in diff: it reads `ret := entryPointAssetsCache` under RLock, returns only if `cfg.Env != setting.Dev && ret != nil`, then unconditionally `entryPointAssetsCacheMu.Lock(); defer entryPointAssetsCacheMu.Unlock()` with no second check shown before starting recomputation (`var err error; var result *dtos.EntryPointAssets`). This can cause redundant recomputation and last-writer-wins overwrites.

Cleanup loop now logs at Error level during normal, successful control flow (false error reporting + log spam). Evidence: in xorm_store.go, after successfully fetching IDs (err checked), it still calls r.log.Error("Annotations to clean by time", ..., "err", err) where err is nil; similarly logs "cleaned annotations by time" with r.log.Error and "err" set to y (often nil). Same pattern for "Annotations to clean by count" and tag cleanup ("Tags to clean" / "cleaned tags").

Mode3 Create: on Storage.Create error it records legacy duration instead of storage duration. Evidence in pkg/apiserver/rest/dualwriter_mode3.go: `startStorage := time.Now(); created, err := d.Storage.Create(...); if err != nil { ... d.recordLegacyDuration(true, mode3Str, options.Kind, method, startStorage) ... }` (should be recordStorageDuration for a storage operation).

Mode3 Update: on Storage.Update error it records legacy duration instead of storage duration. Evidence: `startStorage := time.Now(); res, async, err := d.Storage.Update(...); if err != nil { ... d.recordLegacyDuration(true, mode3Str, options.Kind, method, startStorage) ... }`.

Mode3 Delete: success path records storage duration using `name` instead of `options.Kind`, causing mislabeled metrics. Evidence: after successful delete: `d.recordStorageDuration(false, mode3Str, name, method, startStorage)` while error path uses `options.Kind`.

Mode3 DeleteCollection: legacy async goroutine records storage duration instead of legacy duration (wrong metrics function). Evidence: in goroutine after `d.Legacy.DeleteCollection(...)`: `d.recordStorageDuration(err != nil, mode3Str, options.Kind, method, startLegacy)` (should be recordLegacyDuration).
Mode3 Delete: sets klog context with `d.Log` instead of the enriched `log` (drops name/kind/method fields). Evidence: `log := d.Log.WithValues(...); ctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, d.Log)`.

dualwriter_mode1_test.go: removed local `p := prometheus.NewRegistry()` but still passes `p` to NewDualWriter, causing undefined identifier compile error. Evidence: `dw := NewDualWriter(Mode1, ls, us, p)` with `p` initialization deleted in the diff.

dualwriter_mode3_test.go: multiple tests call `NewDualWriter(..., p)` without defining `p` in scope, causing compile errors. Evidence: TestMode3_Create/List/Delete/DeleteCollection/Update use `dw := NewDualWriter(Mode3, ls, us, p)`; only TestMode3_Get defines `p := prometheus.NewRegistry()`.

useAbilities.ts: useIsGrafanaPromRuleEditable references `ctx` but no `ctx` is defined/imported in scope, causing a runtime ReferenceError when the hook runs. Evidence: `const canEditGrafanaRules = ctx.hasPermissionInMetadata(...)` and `const canRemoveGrafanaRules = ctx.hasPermissionInMetadata(...)` inside `useIsGrafanaPromRuleEditable`.

useAbilities.ts: useAllGrafanaPromRuleAbilities calls `useGrafanaRulesSilenceSupport()` and `useCanSilenceInFolder(...)` but neither function is imported or defined in this file, causing compile-time errors. Evidence: `const silenceSupported = useGrafanaRulesSilenceSupport();` and `const canSilenceInFolder = useCanSilenceInFolder(rule?.folderUid);` with no corresponding imports/definitions in the diff.

`request.filters` is passed into `datasource.applyTemplateVariables(...)` in both `runSplitQuery` and `runShardSplitQuery`, but `DataQueryRequest` does not generally guarantee a `filters` field. This can result in `undefined` being passed where filters are expected, potentially breaking/ignoring ad-hoc filters or causing runtime errors inside `applyTemplateVariables` if it assumes an array/object. Evidence: `...applyTemplateVariables(query, request.scopedVars, request.filters)` in `querySplitting.ts` and `shardQuerySplitting.ts`.

updateDevice uses hard-coded `?` SQL placeholders regardless of DB type, unlike CreateOrUpdateDevice which switches by DB type. This will break on Postgres when the device limit path is hit. Evidence: pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/anonstore/database.go `updateDevice`: `const query = \`UPDATE anon_device SET ... client_ip = ?, ... WHERE device_id = ? AND updated_at BETWEEN ? AND ?\`` and executed via `dbSession.Exec(args...)` with no DB-type adaptation.

Anonymous.Authenticate now fails authentication when the device limit is reached by returning the TagDevice error, effectively denying anonymous access once the limit is hit. Previously tagging was async/best-effort and did not block auth. Evidence: pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/client.go changed from goroutine+log-only to synchronous call and `if errors.Is(err, anonstore.ErrDeviceLimitReached) { return nil, err }`.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: I18n.ensure_loaded! cache never marks locales as loaded, so ensure_loaded! will reload the same locale repeatedly. Evidence: lib/freedom_patches/translate_accelerator.rb adds `def ensure_loaded!(locale); @loaded_locales ||= []; load_locale locale unless @loaded_locales.include?(locale); end` but never appends `locale` to `@loaded_locales` after loading, so `include?` will remain false unless `load_locale` mutates `@loaded_locales` (not shown in this diff).
https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/: MetricsMiddleware is stateful and incorrectly shared across decorations: NewMetricsMiddleware creates a single *MetricsMiddleware (imw := newMetricsMiddleware(...)) and returns it for every client decoration while mutating imw.next inside the returned closure (imw.next = next; return imw). This can cause cross-talk (requests routed to the wrong next) and data races if the middleware factory is used to decorate multiple clients concurrently or sequentially.

ContextualLoggerMiddleware dereferences req without nil checks and can panic: QueryData/CallResource/CheckHealth/CollectMetrics all do `req.PluginContext` (e.g., `ctx = instrumentContext(ctx, endpointQueryData, req.PluginContext)`) without guarding `req == nil`, unlike LoggerMiddleware which explicitly checks for nil requests.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua:
EmbeddableHost before_validation can raise NoMethodError when host is nil: app/models/embeddable_host.rb calls `self.host.sub!` unconditionally in `before_validation`, so creating/updating a record with missing/blank host will crash before validations run.
Evidence:
```rb
before_validation do
  self.host.sub!(/^https?:\/\//, '')
  self.host.sub!(/\/.*$/, '')
end
```

Admin::EmbeddableHostsController#update and #destroy can crash when the record is not found: both actions fetch with `.where(id: params[:id]).first` and then call methods on the result without nil checks. `update` passes possibly-nil `host` into `save_host` which assigns `host.host = ...`; `destroy` calls `host.destroy`.
Evidence:
```rb
def update
  host = EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first
  save_host(host) # host can be nil
end

def destroy
  host = EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first
  host.destroy     # host can be nil
end
```

Migration inserts embeddable_hosts using unescaped string interpolation, which will break on hosts containing quotes and is unsafe: db/migrate/20150818190757_create_embeddable_hosts.rb interpolates `h` directly into SQL.
Evidence:
```rb
execute "INSERT INTO embeddable_hosts (host, category_id, created_at, updated_at) VALUES ('#{h}', #{category_id}, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)"
```

desktop/topic-post.scss: In nav.post-controls, the reply username link color’s light-theme branch was changed from scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%) to dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%), ...). This materially changes the light theme styling and appears to be an incorrect constant (likely copy/paste). Evidence: @@ -288.. a { font-weight: bold; - color: scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%); + color: dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 30%)); }

mobile/modal.scss: .custom-message-length light-theme branch was changed from scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%) to dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%), ...), significantly darkening the text in light theme and inconsistent with the prior value. Evidence: @@ -99.. - color: scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%); + color: dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 70%));

mobile/topic-post.scss: a.star h3 light-theme branch was changed from scale-color($primary, $lightness: 20%) to dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%), ...), a substantial light-theme behavior change unrelated to the stated dark-theme fix. Evidence: @@ -179.. - color: scale-color($primary, $lightness: 20%); + color: dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 50%));

desktop/user.scss and mobile/user.scss: .group-member-info .name light-theme branch was changed from scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%) to dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%), ...), materially changing light-theme emphasis for names. Evidence: desktop/user.scss @@ -519.. - color: scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%); + color: dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 50%)); and mobile/user.scss @@ -494.. same change.

Admin group pagination totalPages is off-by-one: totalPages uses `Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1`, which overcounts when `user_count` is an exact multiple of `limit` (e.g. 100 users, limit 50 => returns 3 instead of 2). Evidence: app/assets/javascripts/admin/controllers/admin-group.js.es6 `totalPages: function() { ... return Math.floor(this.get("user_count") / this.get("limit")) + 1; }`.

Admin group pagination next() can set offset to `user_count`, producing an empty page request (offset equal to total). Evidence: app/assets/javascripts/admin/controllers/admin-group.js.es6 `offset = Math.min(group.get("offset") + group.get("limit"), group.get("user_count"));` then `group.findMembers()`; server uses offset directly.

Spec uses wrong HTTP verb for remove_member: test calls `xhr :put, :remove_member` but route is defined as DELETE. Evidence: spec/controllers/admin/groups_controller_spec.rb `xhr :put, :remove_member, ...`; config/routes.rb `delete "members" => "groups#remove_member"`.

Public group members template iterates `members`, but the route only sets `model` and never sets a `members` property on the controller; unless the template is explicitly bound to `model.members`, this will render nothing. Evidence: app/assets/javascripts/discourse/routes/group-members.js.es6 `controller.set("model", model); model.findMembers();` and app/assets/javascripts/discourse/templates/group/members.hbs changed to `{{#each m in members}}`.

Admin group member item template references `automatic` but that property is not provided in the item view context, so the remove link will incorrectly appear even for automatic groups. Evidence: app/assets/javascripts/admin/templates/group_member.hbs `{{#unless automatic}}<a ...>{{/unless}}` while app/assets/javascripts/admin/views/group-member.js.es6 defines only classNames/templateName and does not supply `automatic`.

Client shows incorrect/misleading error message for blocked/not_allowed emails: create_account_controller caches rejected emails and, when an email is in rejectedEmails, it returns a failed validation with reason I18n.t('user.email.invalid') regardless of the actual server-side error (blocked vs not_allowed). Evidence: create_account_controller.js adds `rejectedEmails: Em.A([])`, checks `if (this.get('rejectedEmails').contains(email)) { ... reason: I18n.t('user.email.invalid') }`, and pushes the email on any server email error via `if (result.errors && result.errors.email && result.values) { ... pushObject(result.values.email) }`.

Email domain restriction regex is incorrect/unsafe: it only escapes '.' and interpolates the entire SiteSetting string directly into a Regexp without anchoring, so it can match substrings like `@good.com.evil.com` and can misbehave if the setting contains regex metacharacters. Evidence: lib/validators/email_validator.rb `domains = setting.gsub('.', '\.')` then `regexp = Regexp.new("@(#{domains})", true)` (no end anchor; no escaping of other metacharacters).

BlockedEmail matching is case-sensitive and does not normalize emails, allowing bypass by changing case and also allowing duplicate rows differing only by case. Evidence: app/models/blocked_email.rb uses `BlockedEmail.where(email: email).first` with no downcasing/ILIKE; model validation is `uniqueness: true` (case-sensitive by default) and DB index is `add_index :blocked_emails, :email, unique: true` (also case-sensitive).

align-items mixin emits an invalid/typo MS vendor property `-ms-align-items`, which is not a real CSS property (IE10/11 use `-ms-flex-align`). Evidence in diff: `@mixin align-items($alignment) { ... -ms-flex-align: $alignment;  -ms-align-items: $alignment; align-items:$alignment; }` in `app/assets/stylesheets/common/foundation/mixins.scss`.

inline-flex mixin uses an invalid/unsupported display value `display: -moz-inline-box;` (old Firefox used `-moz-box`, not `-moz-inline-box`), so that declaration will be ignored. Evidence in diff: `@mixin inline-flex() { ... display: -moz-inline-box; ... }` in `app/assets/stylesheets/common/foundation/mixins.scss`.

order mixin incorrectly maps modern `order` to legacy `-webkit-box-ordinal-group`/`-moz-box-ordinal-group` without the required +1 offset (legacy ordinal-group is 1-based). This will produce wrong ordering in legacy box implementations. Evidence in diff: `@mixin order($val) { -webkit-box-ordinal-group: $val; -moz-box-ordinal-group: $val; ... order: $val; }` and usage `@include order(3);` in `app/assets/stylesheets/common/base/header.scss`.

`website_name` can raise `URI::InvalidURIError` because only the first `URI(website.to_s)` call is rescued; later calls are unguarded. Evidence: `website_host = URI(website.to_s).host rescue nil` then later `website_host + URI(website.to_s).path` (and similar) without rescue, so an invalid user-provided website can 500 during serialization.

`website_name` can return nil for scheme-less URLs even when `website` is present, causing the website label to disappear. Evidence: `website_host = URI(website.to_s).host rescue nil` then `return if website_host.nil?`; for inputs like `example.com/user`, `URI(...).host` is nil so `website_name` becomes nil while `include_website_name` would still include the attribute because `website.present?`.

Enum/default mismatch for posts.cook_method: Post.cook_methods is Enum.new(:regular, :raw_html) so :regular=0 and :raw_html=1, but the migration adds posts.cook_method with default: 1. This makes all posts default to :raw_html and Post#cook will `return raw` (skipping the cooking pipeline) for every post unless explicitly set. Evidence: app/models/post.rb adds `Enum.new(:regular, :raw_html)` and `return raw if cook_method == Post.cook_methods[:raw_html]`; db/migrate/20131219203905_add_cook_method_to_posts.rb sets `default: 1`.

EmbedController spec is inconsistent with controller behavior: controller enqueues a background job `Jobs.enqueue(:retrieve_topic, ...)` when no embed exists, but the spec expects `TopicRetriever.new(...).retrieve` to be called. This will fail tests and indicates a real contract mismatch. Evidence: app/controllers/embed_controller.rb uses `Jobs.enqueue(:retrieve_topic, ...)`; spec/controllers/embed_controller_spec.rb expects `TopicRetriever.expects(:new)... retriever.expects(:retrieve)`.

embed layout uses an invalid postMessage targetOrigin: it passes the full referer URL (`request.referer`) as targetOrigin, but `postMessage` requires an origin (scheme+host+port) or '*'. Passing a full URL (with path/query) can throw and prevent resize messaging. Evidence: app/views/layouts/embed.html.erb: `parent.postMessage(..., '<%= request.referer %>');`.

Client-side upload size validation is now hard-coded to 10MB, ignoring the per-type site setting `Discourse.SiteSettings['max_' + type + '_size_kb']`. This will incorrectly allow/block uploads and show wrong limits/messages when admins configure different limits or for non-image types. Evidence: `var maxSizeKB = Discourse.SiteSettings['max_' + type + '_size_kb'];` changed to `var maxSizeKB = 10 * 1024; // 10MB` in `utilities.js`.

The 413 (entity too large) client-side error handler now hard-codes the displayed max size to 10MB instead of using `Discourse.SiteSettings.max_image_size_kb`, so the user-facing error message can be wrong when the configured limit differs. Evidence: in `utilities.js` case 413, `var maxSizeKB = Discourse.SiteSettings.max_image_size_kb;` changed to `var maxSizeKB = 10 * 1024; // 10 MB`.

In `OptimizedImage`, `self.downsize` is defined twice with different arity; in Ruby the later definition overrides the earlier one, so any existing callers using the old 4-arg signature `(from, to, max_width, max_height, opts={})` will raise `ArgumentError` at runtime. Evidence: `optimized_image.rb` contains both `def self.downsize(from, to, max_width, max_height, opts={})` and later `def self.downsize(from, to, dimensions, opts={})`.

TopicsController#unsubscribe can raise NoMethodError when no TopicUser row exists for the user/topic. The action does `tu = TopicUser.find_by(...)` and then immediately dereferences `tu.notification_level` and calls `tu.save!` without a nil check/creation path.
Evidence (app/controllers/topics_controller.rb):
`tu = TopicUser.find_by(user_id: current_user.id, topic_id: params[:topic_id])`
`if tu.notification_level > TopicUser.notification_levels[:regular] ... end`
`tu.save!`

Email notification template renders an invalid CSS class attribute by including a leading dot in the class name, so the class will literally be `.previous-discussion` and won’t match expected selectors. Evidence (app/views/email/notification.html.erb): `

<%= t "user_notifications.previous_discussion" %>

`
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/2: CalendarCacheRepositoryMock does not match the updated ICalendarCacheRepository signature: interface requires getCacheStatusByCredentialIds(credentialIds: number[]), but mock implements async getCacheStatusByCredentialIds() with no parameter. This will fail TypeScript interface conformance/compilation. Evidence: calendar-cache.repository.interface.ts adds method with (credentialIds: number[]); calendar-cache.repository.mock.ts defines getCacheStatusByCredentialIds() without args.

GoogleCalendarService calls SelectedCalendarRepository.updateManyByCredentialId with an empty update object `{}`. Prisma updateMany requires a non-empty `data` payload; passing `{}` is invalid and will throw at runtime, so this code path can break availability fetching. Evidence: CalendarService.ts `updateManyByCredentialId(this.credential.id, {});` and selectedCalendar.ts `prisma.selectedCalendar.updateMany({ where: { credentialId }, data })`.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/8: packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/slots.ts: checkIfIsAvailable() compares Dayjs objects by reference equality instead of comparing instants, so the `start == end` override check will never be true. Evidence: `if (dayjs(date.start).add(utcOffset, "minutes") === dayjs(date.end).add(utcOffset, "minutes")) { return true; }`

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/slots.ts: working-hours bounds check has a copy/paste bug: `end` is computed from `slotStartTime` instead of using the slot end time (or at least `slotEndTime`), so it never validates that the slot duration fits within `workingHour.endTime`. Evidence: `const start = slotStartTime.hour() * 60 + slotStartTime.minute(); const end = slotStartTime.hour() * 60 + slotStartTime.minute(); if (start < workingHour.startTime || end > workingHour.endTime) { return true; }`

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/slots.ts: date override logic is inverted: the `.find(...)` predicate returns true for non-overlapping cases (slot ends before override start OR slot starts after override end), and the outer `if (find(...)) return false;` marks those non-overlapping slots as unavailable. Evidence: inside `dateOverrides.find(...)` it returns true when `slotEndTime.isBefore(...date.start...) || slotEndTime.isSame(...date.start...)` or when `slotStartTime.isAfter(...date.end...)`, then outside: `// slot is not within the date override
return false;`

packages/lib/slots.ts: getSlots() uses an undefined identifier `timeZone` when computing `inviteeUtcOffset`, which will fail compilation or throw at runtime. Evidence: `const inviteeUtcOffset = dayjs(override.start.toString()).tz(timeZone).utcOffset();` (no `timeZone` defined in the shown scope).
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/1: deleteMany() now deletes WorkflowReminder rows for ANY method when retryCount > 1 due to the OR branch lacking a method filter. Evidence: deleteMany where: { OR: [ { method: WorkflowMethods.SMS, scheduledDate: { lte: dayjs().toISOString() } }, { retryCount: { gt: 1 } } ] } — the retryCount condition applies to all reminders, not just SMS, causing unintended deletions (including future reminders).

Cleanup contradicts the comment/intent by not scoping the 'past current date' deletion to scheduled reminders. Evidence: comment says "delete all scheduled sms reminders..." but the filter only checks method: SMS and scheduledDate <= now (no scheduled: true), so it will also delete unscheduled SMS reminders whose scheduledDate is in the past.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/2: Constructor type now allows `{ scope: "team" }` without `teamId`, but internal logic requires it; Zod validation will fail and silently set `this.options = null`, causing authorization to return `NOTHING_CONDITION` (deny all) unexpectedly. Evidence: `export type InsightsBookingServicePublicOptions = { scope: "user" | "org" | "team"; userId: number; orgId: number; teamId?: number; };` + `this.options = validation.success ? validation.data : null;` + `if (!this.options) { return NOTHING_CONDITION; }`. This is a concrete behavioral bug (valid TS input can unexpectedly deny access).
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/1:
createOAuthAppCredential has an incorrect relative import after the file move: it imports decodeOAuthState from `../oauth/decodeOAuthState` even though it is already in the `oauth` folder, resulting in a non-existent `oauth/oauth/...` path.

Evidence (packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/createOAuthAppCredential.ts):
```ts
import { decodeOAuthState } from "../oauth/decodeOAuthState";
```
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/7:
scheduleEmailReminders.ts cancellation query does not restrict to email reminders, so it will attempt to cancel non-email (e.g., SMS) reminders via SendGrid.
Evidence: packages/features/ee/workflows/api/scheduleEmailReminders.ts
- remindersToCancel where-clause only uses { cancelled: true, scheduledDate: ... } and does not filter by method/email, but the cancellation uses SendGrid scheduled_sends API.

deleteScheduledEmailReminder(immediateDelete=true) returns after cancelling in SendGrid and does not delete or mark the WorkflowReminder row, leaving stale DB records.
Evidence: packages/features/ee/workflows/lib/reminders/emailReminderManager.ts
- if (immediateDelete) { await client.request(...cancel...); return; } // no prisma delete/update

handleNewBooking.ts uses deleteScheduledEmailReminder(..., true) for rescheduled bookings, which (per implementation) cancels externally and returns without deleting/updating the DB row, leaving old reminders persisted.
Evidence: packages/features/bookings/lib/handleNewBooking.ts
- deleteScheduledEmailReminder(reminder.id, reminder.referenceId, true);
And emailReminderManager.ts immediateDelete path returns without DB change.

Multiple call sites fire deleteScheduledEmailReminder/deleteScheduledSMSReminder without awaiting, so the request/procedure can complete before cancellations/DB writes finish; errors can become unhandled rejections and state can diverge.
Evidence examples in diff:
- handleCancelBooking.ts: updatedBookings.forEach(...){ deleteScheduledEmailReminder(...); deleteScheduledSMSReminder(...); } and these promises are not included in Promise.all.
- bookingsRouter (viewer/bookings.tsx): workflowReminders.forEach(...){ deleteScheduled*... } and the previous await Promise.all(remindersToDelete) was removed.
- workflowsRouter (viewer/workflows.tsx): several forEach loops call deleteScheduled* without await; additionally remindersToUpdate.forEach(async ...) is not awaited.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/1: Compile/runtime error: `AttendeeScheduledEmail` is referenced in `sendAddGuestsEmails` but is not imported in `packages/emails/email-manager.ts` in this diff (only `AttendeeAddGuestsEmail`/`OrganizerAddGuestsEmail` are newly imported). Evidence: `new AttendeeScheduledEmail(...)` is used, but there is no corresponding import added for it in the shown import section.

Case-sensitivity bug in server-side guest filtering/blacklist: blacklist entries are lowercased, but `guest` is not normalized before comparisons, so case-variant emails can bypass blacklist and duplicate checks. Evidence (addGuests.handler.ts):
```ts
const blacklistedGuestEmails = ...map((email) => email.toLowerCase());
...
!booking.attendees.some((attendee) => guest === attendee.email) &&
!blacklistedGuestEmails.includes(guest)
```

OrganizerAddGuestsEmail can throw at runtime: subject uses `this.calEvent.attendees[0].name` without guarding for an empty attendees array. Evidence (organizer-add-guests-email.ts):
```ts
name: this.calEvent.attendees[0].name,
```

BackupCode component is misnamed: `apps/web/components/auth/BackupCode.tsx` default-exports a function named `TwoFactor` (`export default function TwoFactor({ center = true }) { ... }`). This is a concrete naming/contract bug (misleading stack traces and easy confusion/collision with the real `TwoFactor` component) while it is imported/used as `BackupCode` elsewhere (e.g., `import BackupCode from "@components/auth/BackupCode";`).

Enable 2FA setup API can crash when `CALENDSO_ENCRYPTION_KEY` is missing: `apps/web/pages/api/auth/two-factor/totp/setup.ts` calls `symmetricEncrypt(..., process.env.CALENDSO_ENCRYPTION_KEY)` for both `backupCodes` and `twoFactorSecret` without any guard, unlike other endpoints that explicitly check for the key. If the env var is undefined, this can throw at runtime and break 2FA setup.

GoogleCalendarService.updateEvent/deleteEvent: broken fallback calendar selection. In updateEvent, `const selectedCalendar = externalCalendarId ? externalCalendarId : event.destinationCalendar?.find((cal) => cal.externalId === externalCalendarId)?.externalId;` and in deleteEvent similarly uses `find((cal) => cal.externalId === externalCalendarId)` in the branch where `externalCalendarId` is falsy. This will almost always return undefined, causing the code to fall back to `"primary"` and update/delete the wrong calendar when a destination calendar exists.

EventManager Meet fallback can throw when destinationCalendar is empty/null. Code: `const [mainHostDestinationCalendar] = evt.destinationCalendar ?? []; if (evt.location === MeetLocationType && mainHostDestinationCalendar.integration !== "google_calendar") { ... }` dereferences `.integration` without optional chaining; if `evt.destinationCalendar` is `[]` or `null`, `mainHostDestinationCalendar` is undefined and this will crash.

requestReschedule.handler has a copy/paste bug: `destinationCalendar: bookingToReschedule?.destinationCalendar || bookingToReschedule?.destinationCalendar,` repeats the same expression, so it never falls back to any other value and will produce `[]` when destinationCalendar is missing.

Calendar interface change not fully implemented: `packages/types/Calendar.d.ts` changes `createEvent` to `createEvent(event: CalendarEvent, credentialId: number)`, but Office365CalendarService and LarkCalendarService still declare `async createEvent(event: CalendarEvent)` (no `credentialId` param). This is a real interface/implementation mismatch that will break type-checking and can cause inconsistent runtime behavior when callers pass the new second argument.

EventManager.updateAllCalendarEvents can call updateEvent with an undefined credential. In the loop, when `reference.credentialId` is set but the credential is not found in memory and DB lookup doesn't yield a usable credential, it still does `result.push(updateEvent(credential, event, bookingRefUid, calenderExternalId));` where `credential` may be undefined, likely causing a runtime error inside updateEvent/getCalendar.

handleNewBooking loadUsers can return undefined for users array. In the non-dynamic branch it returns `users.length ? users : eventType.users;` but `eventType.users` can be undefined; later code does `users.slice(1)` unconditionally, which would throw if `users` is undefined.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/8:
Unawaited async side effects: multiple places changed to `forEach(async ...)` and the returned Promises are ignored, so calendar/video deletions may still be in-flight (or fail) after the surrounding function continues/returns; errors inside the async callback also won’t be caught by the surrounding `try/catch`. Evidence in diff:
- `packages/app-store/vital/lib/reschedule.ts`: `bookingRefsFiltered.forEach(async (bookingRef) => { ... const calendar = await getCalendar(...); return calendar?.deleteEvent(...); ... })`
- `packages/app-store/wipemycalother/lib/reschedule.ts`: same pattern
- `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx`: same pattern
- `packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts`: recurring updates loop uses `.forEach(async (credential) => { const calendar = await getCalendar(credential); ... })` and is not awaited.

Breaking runtime/type behavior: `getCalendar` is now async, but `getCalendarCredentials` still assigns `const calendar = getCalendar(credential);` (no `await`), so `calendar` becomes a Promise. Only one consumer (`getConnectedCalendars`) was updated to `await item.calendar`; any other consumer of `calendarCredentials` that treats `item.calendar` as a `Calendar | null` will misbehave (Promise is truthy) or throw when calling methods. Evidence in diff: `packages/core/CalendarManager.ts` `getCalendarCredentials` still does `const calendar = getCalendar(credential);` while `getConnectedCalendars` now does `const calendar = await item.calendar;`.
